feed.title: Data me!
feed.subtitle: Blogs on machine learning and data in general.

entry.title: PIL converts NaNs images to zero value images

link.title: PIL converts NaNs images to zero value images

entry.summary: I was having problems with missing information in my problem. There were missing images which were read as NaNs image shape matrixes but at the end they were fed to the CNN.

entry.title: Count NaN values that are not NaN in Pandas DataFrame

link.title: Count NaN values that are not NaN in Pandas DataFrame

entry.summary: I am working with a dataset that contains lots of missing values. However, when a value is missed it is not represented as NaN but as “–”. In order to find how many missing values there are in an specific column I cannot use typical Pandas functions. Henceforth, I have worked it out by count the number of appereances of this character in the column and substract it to the total number of entries in the column:

entry.title: How to set Python3 as default version on MacOS

link.title: How to set Python3 as default version on MacOS

entry.summary: In order not to have to type everytime you want to run Python 3 python3 I wanted to link to the default version of Python. Then you need to use the following commands:

entry.title: Moving from Tensorflow 2.0 to Pytorch
entry.summary: I was having many issues with GPU memory management in a Transfer Learning problem when using Tensorflow 2.0 with Keras module. Even though I was using Inception V3 model with all the weights of the architecture freezed, the code ran out of memory. The weights of the model occupied only 98 MB therefore that wasn’t the problem. Input shape was (299, 299, 3) so not huge images were being used, but I couldn’t use a batch size higher than 16.
